statutoryBodies.name: Schools Examinations Advisory Committee
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Established under Section 3. Constituted by the Minister in accordance with regulations made under Section 22(2)(e). The Education Ordinance's parallel School Examinations Advisory Council provides additional precedent: the Commissioner of Examinations serves as Chairman (non-voting) with a prescribed number of members appointed by the Minister. Current operational status unverified.

exOfficio.note: Chairman — presides at meetings but is not entitled to vote (per Education Ordinance precedent)

nominated.note: Prescribed number of members — exact count defined in regulations under Section 22(2)(e), not in the Act itself

composition.termLength: Defined in ministerial regulations under Section 22(2)(e)

meetings.chairperson: Commissioner of Examinations (non-voting); if absent, members elect one of their number to preside (per Education Ordinance precedent)
meetings.reporting: Advisory to the Commissioner and Minister of Education

powers: Consulted by the Director-General of Education on syllabuses and books prescribed for General Education Examinations (Section 4)
Advisory role on schools examination policy and standards
Provides recommendations to the Commissioner regarding examination conduct
dataGaps: Exact composition prescribed in ministerial regulations — regulations text not publicly available
Current membership and whether the committee is actively convened is unverified
Meeting frequency and records not publicly accessible
Relationship with National Education Commission and NIE unclear
Education Ordinance provisions used as precedent — may differ from regulations under this Act

statutoryBodies.name: Technical Examinations Advisory Committee
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Established under Section 3 alongside the Schools Examinations Advisory Committee. Constituted by the Minister in accordance with regulations under Section 22(2)(e). Advises on Technical Education Examinations (excluding Higher Education).

exOfficio.note: Likely Chairman (by analogy with Schools Examinations Advisory Committee)

nominated.note: Prescribed number — exact count defined in regulations under Section 22(2)(e)

composition.termLength: Defined in ministerial regulations under Section 22(2)(e)

meetings.chairperson: Commissioner of Examinations (presumed, by analogy with Schools committee)
meetings.reporting: Advisory to the Commissioner and Minister of Education

powers: Consulted by the Director-General of Education on syllabuses and books prescribed for Technical Education Examinations (Section 4)
Advisory role on technical examination policy and standards
Provides recommendations to the Commissioner regarding technical examination conduct
dataGaps: Exact composition prescribed in ministerial regulations — regulations text not publicly available
Current membership and whether the committee is actively convened is unverified
Relationship with technical/vocational training institutions unclear
Whether this committee covers NAITA or TVEC examinations is unknown

statutoryBodies.name: Department of Examinations
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Non-ministerial government department under the purview of the Ministry of Education. Headed by the Commissioner-General of Examinations. Located in Pelawatta, Battaramulla. Conducts O/L, A/L, and all government examinations.
powers: Organise and conduct General Education Examinations (O/L, A/L)
Organise and conduct Technical Education Examinations (excluding Higher Education)
Conduct Government department examinations and Government-sponsored body examinations
Issue certificates and awards to successful candidates
Commissioner has absolute discretion on candidate rules, disciplinary control over examiners/invigilators (Section 20)
Cancel or impound certificates for rule violations (Section 20)
Debar candidates from future examinations for serious malpractice
Administer Oath of Secrecy for all examination staff (Section 21)
dataGaps: Detailed internal organisational structure not publicly documented in the Act
Number of Deputy/Assistant Commissioners not specified in legislation
When title changed from 'Commissioner' to 'Commissioner-General' — instrument unknown

amendments.summary: Extended delegation of Commissioner's powers to Regional Directors of Education and Chief Education Officers, enabling decentralised examination administration.
amendments.details: Amended Section 2 to allow the Commissioner to delegate powers and duties not only to Departmental staff (Deputy Commissioner, Assistant Commissioner, Superintendent of Examinations) but also to Regional Directors of Education and Chief Education Officers. This was a significant administrative reform enabling decentralised management of examination centres across the island, reflecting the growing scale of public examinations. Terminology was also updated to align with changes in the education administrative structure.

timeline.details: Act No. 25 of 1968 certified on June 16, 1968. Established the legal framework for the conduct of public examinations and prescribed punishments for examination offences. Commissioner of Examinations designated as administrative head.
Decentralised examination administration by extending delegation powers to Regional Directors of Education and Chief Education Officers.
Department of Examinations brought under enhanced financial and performance audit requirements.

tiers.name: Commissioner-General of Examinations
tiers.relationship: Administrative head of the Department of Examinations; absolute discretion over examination conduct (Section 20)

tiers.name: Deputy / Assistant Commissioners & Superintendents
tiers.relationship: Delegated powers from Commissioner under Section 2

tiers.name: Regional Directors of Education / Chief Education Officers
tiers.relationship: Delegated powers from Commissioner under Section 2 (added by 1976 Amendment)

tiers.name: Schools Examinations Advisory Committee
tiers.relationship: Advisory body for General Education Examinations — consulted on syllabuses (Section 3)

tiers.name: Technical Examinations Advisory Committee
tiers.relationship: Advisory body for Technical Education Examinations — consulted on syllabuses (Section 3)

currentReplacement.national: Department of Examinations (non-ministerial department, Pelawatta, Battaramulla)
currentReplacement.regional: Delegated to Regional Directors of Education and Chief Education Officers for examination centre management
